name: Briefcase (BeeWare Cross-Platform App Packaging)

auth.notes: CLI tool; Apple/Microsoft signing certificates required for notarized distribution

performance.notes: Build tool; runtime performance same as PyInstaller/native

agent_readiness.notes: BeeWare tool for creating native app installers across platforms. briefcase new (scaffolds project). briefcase dev (run in dev mode). briefcase build (build for current platform). briefcase run (run packaged app). briefcase package (create installer: .msi, .dmg, .deb). Targets: macOS (.app/.dmg), Windows (.msi), Linux (.AppImage/.deb/.rpm), iOS, Android, Web. Works with Toga (BeeWare native UI) but supports any UI. pyproject.toml config. Bundles Python interpreter. Integrated with Toga cross-platform widget toolkit.

gotchas: Native UI requires Toga — limited widget set vs Qt
Platform-specific build requirements (Xcode for macOS, Visual Studio for Windows)
Slower development iteration than PyInstaller for desktop-only apps
Best suited for BeeWare/Toga ecosystem — mixing with other UI frameworks is complex
best_when: Cross-platform mobile + desktop apps from single Python codebase
When iOS/Android Python apps are the goal
avoid_when: Desktop-only distribution (PyInstaller or Nuitka are simpler)
When mobile support is not needed (overhead not justified)
Production iOS/Android apps requiring full native features
summary: Briefcase is BeeWare's tool for packaging Python apps as native installers. briefcase build creates .app/.dmg (macOS), .msi (Windows), .AppImage/.deb (Linux). briefcase build ios creates iOS app. pyproject.toml configuration. Bundles Python interpreter. Part of BeeWare ecosystem alongside Toga native UI. BSD-3. Best for cross-platform apps targeting mobile+desktop from one Python codebase.
